world.description: US News: US Vice President JD Vance advises the Vatican to focus on moral issues and refrain from interfering in American politics, amidst a public dispute between President Trump and Pope Leo XIV regarding the ongoing conflict in Iran.

world.title: From Fragmented Markets to Regional Powerhouse: Africa’s Path to Economic Growth
world.description: Africa’s trade has not driven growth because it relies on raw exports and fragmented markets, despite high openness. The report argues that deeper regional integration, better systems, and stronger value chains are key to unlocking industrial growth and prosperity.

business.title: Qantas lifts fuel cost forecast as Middle East war jolts oil markets
business.description: Australia's Qantas Airways said on Tuesday it has raised its fuel cost outlook and has not started its planned share buyback, citing sharply higher and volatile jet fuel prices after the war in the Middle East cut oil supply.

business.title: Iran Oil Hoard at Sea Shields China’s Refiners From US Blockade
business.description: A hoard of Iranian crude on tankers at sea and robust onshore stockpiles in China will provide a cushion for the nation’s independent refiners should a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz choke off flows.

business.title: Break-in in Jasin saw seafood stolen, trader claims perp selling them nearby
business.description: JASIN: A seafood trader in Pantai Siring woke up to an unexpected loss when a break-in at her stall saw not cash or equipment stolen, but the very marine produce she relies on to make a living.

science.title: New method boosts AI-driven protein engineering with massive data
science.description: Protein engineering is a field primed for artificial intelligence research. Each protein is made up of amino acids; to optimize a protein function, researchers modify proteins by switching out one of 20 different amino acids for another.

science.title: Lyrids Meteor Shower to Light Up Singapore Skies for 10 Nights This April
science.description: The Lyrids meteor shower will light up Singapore's night sky for ten nights starting April 17-here are tips for the best viewing.

An image taken by an astronaut aboard the International Space Station showed Lake Iro in southern Chad, with [...]

science.title: Lunar soil bears proof of organic matter
science.description: An international research team has identified nitrogen-bearing organic compounds on the surface of moon soil for the first time, providing a new window into how the building blocks of life may have traveled through the early solar system.

science.title: Exoplanet Host Star Shares Elemental Traits with Its Hot Jupiter
science.description: An ultra-hot Jupiter exoplanet orbiting a nearby star gave scientists using the Gemini South telescope a look at how both a star and its hot planet can have similar chemical compositions. The team, led by Arizona State University graduate student Jorge Antonio Sanchez, took spectra of the planet, called WASP-189b, using the Immersion Grating Infrared Spectrograph instrument. The observations measured the abundance of magnesium compared to silicon in the hot planet's atmosphere and allowed the team to compare it to the makeup of its parent star.

science.title: Simulations reveal cold, dusty reality of galaxy formation
science.description: A realistic picture of how galaxies formed and evolved from the first billion years after the Big Bang to the present day has been revealed.

ai.title: Study warns of rising teen dependency on AI companions
ai.description: It's estimated that more than half of all of U.S. teens are regularly using companion chatbots powered by large language models and generative artificial intelligence (AI) technology.

ai.title: Google Gemini now offers free NEET UG mock tests for students in India
ai.description: Google is bringing NEET UG mock tests on Gemini. Now, users can give full-length practice tests by giving a single prompt to the AI. Here is how it works, and what you need to know.

ai.title: China's March exports slow as Iran war wipes out AI-driven gains
ai.description: Chinas export growth slowed sharply in March to 2.5%, a five-month low, as the impact of the Middle East conflict and rising energy costs offset strong demand driven by artificial intelligence-related goods, according to customs data. While exports missed expectations, imports surged 27.8%, signalling resilient domestic demand. The slowdown marks an early test of whether AI-led export momentum can counter the global energy shock triggered by tensions around the Strait of Hormuz, which handles a significant share of global oil and gas flows.
